channel.title: Daily Interview English
channel.description: Daily 5-minute episodes: English patterns, interview frameworks, and speaking practice for tech professionals.
channel.itunes:author: Daily Interview English

item.title: API Gateway Explained (ByteByteGo)
item.description: What an API gateway does: authentication, rate limiting, request routing, protocol translation, and service discovery. How it fits into a microservices architecture.
item.itunes:summary: What an API gateway does: authentication, rate limiting, request routing, protocol translation, and service discovery. How it fits into a microservices architecture.

item.title: How a URL Shortener Works — System Design Walkthrough (ByteByteGo)
item.description: End-to-end system design for a URL shortener: hash functions, base62 encoding, database schema, caching strategy, and scaling to millions of URLs.
item.itunes:summary: End-to-end system design for a URL shortener: hash functions, base62 encoding, database schema, caching strategy, and scaling to millions of URLs.

item.title: Rate Limiter Design — Token Bucket & Leaky Bucket (ByteByteGo)
item.description: Token bucket, leaky bucket, fixed window, sliding window — the four rate limiting algorithms explained. How to scale rate limiters in a distributed system.
item.itunes:summary: Token bucket, leaky bucket, fixed window, sliding window — the four rate limiting algorithms explained. How to scale rate limiters in a distributed system.

item.title: CAP Theorem Simplified (ByteByteGo)
item.description: Why distributed systems must choose between Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance. Clear explanation with real-world database examples (CP vs AP systems).
item.itunes:summary: Why distributed systems must choose between Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance. Clear explanation with real-world database examples (CP vs AP systems).

item.title: Consistent Hashing Explained (ByteByteGo)
item.description: Visual explainer on consistent hashing — how distributed systems assign keys to nodes and minimize redistribution when nodes are added or removed. Core concept for sharding and distributed caches.
item.itunes:summary: Visual explainer on consistent hashing — how distributed systems assign keys to nodes and minimize redistribution when nodes are added or removed. Core concept for sharding and distributed caches.

item.title: Message Queues & Event-Driven Architecture — Patterns & Tradeoffs
item.description: Chris Patterson (MassTransit creator) on message brokers vs event streaming, sagas vs routing slips, transactional outbox pattern, dead-letter queues. Transport-agnostic architectural patterns for distributed systems.
item.itunes:summary: Chris Patterson (MassTransit creator) on message brokers vs event streaming, sagas vs routing slips, transactional outbox pattern, dead-letter queues. Transport-agnostic architectural patterns for distributed systems.

item.title: Rate Limiting & High-Traffic Systems — Real Incident Walkthrough
item.description: Mojtaba Sarooghi (Queue-it) on handling flash traffic: 100K to 100M requests in one minute, cold-start failures, DynamoDB at 200K TPS, rate limiting algorithms, and bot detection (98% of traffic is bots during ticket drops).
item.itunes:summary: Mojtaba Sarooghi (Queue-it) on handling flash traffic: 100K to 100M requests in one minute, cold-start failures, DynamoDB at 200K TPS, rate limiting algorithms, and bot detection (98% of traffic is bots during ticket drops).

item.title: Event Sourcing & CQRS — Architecture Deep Dive
item.description: Martin Dilger (author of Understanding Event Sourcing) explains event stores, projections, CQRS, event versioning, and why Kafka is NOT an event store. Practical adoption strategy: 2-day modeling workshop + 1-day build.
item.itunes:summary: Martin Dilger (author of Understanding Event Sourcing) explains event stores, projections, CQRS, event versioning, and why Kafka is NOT an event store. Practical adoption strategy: 2-day modeling workshop + 1-day build.

item.title: System Design Interviews — The Complete Framework
item.description: Sriram Panyam (ex-Google, ex-LinkedIn) breaks down the system design interview: 5-phase approach, 25 canonical problems, what interviewers look for at each level, and the key mindset shift from buzzword-driven to requirement-driven design. The single best SDI resource.
item.itunes:summary: Sriram Panyam (ex-Google, ex-LinkedIn) breaks down the system design interview: 5-phase approach, 25 canonical problems, what interviewers look for at each level, and the key mindset shift from buzzword-driven to requirement-driven design. The single best SDI resource.

item.description: System design episode: Architecture Patterns II
item.itunes:summary: System design episode: Architecture Patterns II

item.title: Reliability Engineering — Idempotency, Rate Limiting, Observability

item.title: API & Microservices — API Design, Microservices Architecture
item.description: System design episode: API & Microservices

item.title: Data Architecture — Sharding, Replication, Partitioning
item.description: System design episode: Data Architecture

item.title: Infrastructure Essentials — Load Balancing, Caching, CDN, Message Queues

item.title: System Design Fundamentals — CAP, ACID vs BASE, Consensus
item.description: System design episode: System Design Fundamentals
item.itunes:summary: System design episode: System Design Fundamentals

item.title: Thinking Time & Bridging Gaps — Bridging Patterns + I Don't Know Strategies
item.description: Compilation episode: Thinking Time & Bridging Gaps
item.itunes:summary: Compilation episode: Thinking Time & Bridging Gaps

item.title: Emphasis & Elaboration — Impact Patterns + Hook-Flow-Landing
item.description: Compilation episode: Emphasis & Elaboration
item.itunes:summary: Compilation episode: Emphasis & Elaboration

item.title: Self-Description & Career Narrative — Self-Description Patterns + STAR-LA
item.description: Compilation episode: Self-Description & Career Narrative
item.itunes:summary: Compilation episode: Self-Description & Career Narrative

item.title: Stating Opinions & Perspectives — Opinion Patterns + Verbal Signposting
item.description: Compilation episode: Stating Opinions & Perspectives
item.itunes:summary: Compilation episode: Stating Opinions & Perspectives

item.title: Prepared Q&A — 7 Key Interview Questions
item.description: Special practice episode: prepared answers for 7 key interview questions. Tell me about yourself, Why this company, Why leaving current role, Built something from zero, Worked with non-engineers, Security approach, What you're looking for. Each uses STAR-LA or Structure-First with real stories. Includes key numbers quick reference.
item.itunes:summary: Special practice episode: prepared answers for 7 key interview questions. Tell me about yourself, Why this company, Why leaving current role, Built something from zero, Worked with non-engineers, Security approach, What you're looking for. Each uses STAR-LA or Structure-First with real stories. Includes key numbers quick reference.

item.title: Self-Introduction Practice — Present-Past-Future
item.description: Special practice episode: your self-introduction using the Present-Past-Future framework. Covers trading systems experience, legacy migration, early coding agent adoption, vulnerability platform, security tool ($100K+ subscription replacement), internal dev platform, outside interests, and the interview close. Includes section-by-section breakdown, memory anchors, and a 60-second backup.
item.itunes:summary: Special practice episode: your self-introduction using the Present-Past-Future framework. Covers trading systems experience, legacy migration, early coding agent adoption, vulnerability platform, security tool ($100K+ subscription replacement), internal dev platform, outside interests, and the interview close. Includes section-by-section breakdown, memory anchors, and a 60-second backup.
